Title: Biofortified tomatoes provide a new route to vitamin D sufficiency

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin D3 supports human nutrition and health
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mentioned as the other tomato sterol Δ7-reductase gene; not targeted by CRISPR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UVB irradiation relevant for conversion of 7-DHC to vitamin D3
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not directly manipulated; proposed to possibly supplement cholesterol production when Sl7-DR2 is knocked out
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Off-target analysis showed no edits; inference from metabolite outcomes and RT-qPCR context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sl7-DR1 was the only other sterol Δ7-reductase gene considered during CRISPR design. 2. No off-target edits were detected in Sl7-DR1 in Sl7-DR2 mutant lines. 3. The paper suggests Sl7-DR1 or phytosterol-pathway enzymes may partially compensate cholesterol production, but this was not directly proven.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR context; LC-MS metabolite profiling; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important non-target paralog to avoid editing when engineering Sl7-DR2-specific provitamin D3 accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/supporting gene rather than the main experimental target, but functionally relevant to interpretation of flux compensation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin D3 supports human nutrition and health
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels in leaves were consistently ~30% lower in Sl7-DR2 mutants than controls
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not a primary target; only pathway gene whose transcript abundance decreased consistently in Sl7-DR2 mutants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RT-qPCR of pathway genes detected no major compensatory transcriptional changes after Sl7-DR2 knockout. 2. SlC5-SD1 was the only gene showing a consistent ~30% transcript reduction in mutants. 3. This change did not alter the overall conclusion that phytosterol metabolism was largely unaffected.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily useful as a monitoring marker of pathway perturbation rather than a direct engineering target in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive/background gene, not directly functionally validated for vitamin D biofortification in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cholesterol/steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis (duplicate sterol pathway in Solanaceae)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cholesterogenesis branch feeding steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 7-dehydrocholesterol to cholesterol in the specialized cholesterol/SGA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 7-dehydrocholesterol (provitamin D3) and, after UVB conversion, vitamin D3
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; green fruit and fruit flesh/peel; lower levels persist in ripe fruit
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin D3 supports skeletal development and broader steroid-hormone-linked human health
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly associated with leaves and fruit in the duplicate cholesterol/SGA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in leaves and fruit; knockout causes strongest 7-DHC accumulation in leaves and green fruit
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth at 20–22 °C with 16 h light; UVB irradiation (302 nm, 1 h) converts accumulated 7-DHC to vitamin D3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout strongly increases 7-DHC accumulation in leaves and fruit, enables vitamin D3 production after UVB exposure, and reduces α-tomatine/esculeoside A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in five independent alleles; segregation to transgene-free homozygotes; LC-MS sterol profiling; MALDI imaging; UVB conversion assay; RT-qPCR of pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for diverting flux from cholesterol/SGA synthesis toward 7-DHC accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR knockout of Sl7-DR2 caused substantial accumulation of 7-DHC in tomato leaves and green fruit. 2. UVB treatment converted the accumulated 7-DHC into vitamin D3, giving high levels in leaves and measurable levels in fruit. 3. Loss of Sl7-DR2 reduced α-tomatine and esculeoside A without affecting plant growth, development, yield, or major phytosterol metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: U6-III promoter for sgRNAs; constitutive Cas9-expression cassette
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by LC-MS; MALDI imaging; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced steroidal glycoalkaloids (α-tomatine, esculeoside A); cholesterol unexpectedly remained generally higher than WT; no observed growth, development, or yield penalty
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for CRISPR editing in elite tomato cultivars to biofortify provitamin D3/vitamin D3; can be stacked with traits increasing UVB penetration (for example pink tomato y locus); vegetative waste could be used for plant-based vitamin D3 supplement production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No off-target edits detected in Sl7-DR1; phytosterol pathway appeared largely unaffected, supporting pathway duplication and metabolic flexibility in Solanaceae
